subject: ELA
title: Grammar & Parts of Speech
description: Nouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs, and sentence types.

questions.q: Which word is a NOUN in: 'The playful puppy chased the red ball.'
Choose the correct verb: 'She ___ to school every day.'
What part of speech is 'quickly' in 'He ran quickly'?
questions.explanation: 'Quickly' tells HOW he ran — adverb modifies a verb.

questions.q: What type of sentence is: 'Wow, what a beautiful sunset!'
questions.explanation: Strong emotion + exclamation mark = exclamatory sentence.

questions.q: Correct the verb: 'Yesterday, I goed to the park.' Replace 'goed'.
questions.explanation: 'Go' is irregular; its past tense is 'went'.

options: I wanted pizza, but we had pasta.
questions.answer: I wanted pizza, but we had pasta.
questions.explanation: Two independent clauses joined by the conjunction 'but'.
Un- means not/opposite. Example: unhappy = not happy.

questions.q: Which word is a VERB in: 'The bright stars shone in the dark sky.'
questions.explanation: 'Shone' is the action the stars performed = verb.

questions.q: What type of sentence is: 'Please close the door.'
questions.explanation: A command directed at someone = imperative sentence.
